data.title: ESS Tech, Inc. Files 8K - Listing Notice >GWH
data.description:
ESS Tech, Inc. (GWH) filed a Form 8K - Notice of Delisting or Failure to Satisfy a Continued Listing Rule or Standard; Transfer of Listing - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on March 28, 2025.

On March 24, 2025, ESS Tech, Inc. (the "Company") received a written notice (the "Notice") from the New York Stock Exchange (the "NYSE") indicating that the Company is no longer in compliance with NYSE's continued listing standards set forth in Section 802.01B (the "Minimum Market Capitalization Standard") due to the fact that the Company's average global market capitalization over a consecutive 30 trading-day period was less than $50 million and, at the same time, its stockholders' equity was less than $50 million. As described in the Notice, as of March 21, 2025, the Company's 30 trading-day average market capitalization was approximately $47.8 million and its last reported stockholders' equity as of September 30, 2024, was approximately $49.2 million.

In accordance with applicable NYSE procedures, within 45 days from receipt of the Notice, the Company intends to submit a plan to the NYSE advising it of the definitive action(s) the Company has taken, is taking, or plans to take that would bring it into conformity with the Minimum Market Capitalization Standard within 18 months of receipt of the Notice (the "Cure Period"). Upon receipt of such plan, the NYSE will have up to 45 days to evaluate the plan and determine whether the Company has made a reasonable demonstration of its ability to come into conformity with the relevant listing standards within the Cure Period. If the NYSE accepts the Company's plan, the NYSE will review the Company on a quarterly basis to confirm compliance with the plan. If the Company's plan is not accepted, the Company fails to comply with the plan or does not meet the Minimum Market Capitalization Standard at the end of the Cure Period, it will be subject to NYSE's prompt initiation of suspension and delisting procedures.

The Notice has no immediate impact on the listing of the Company's common stock, which will continue to be listed and traded on the NYSE during the Cure Period, subject to the NYSE's acceptance of the Company's plan, the Company's continued compliance with the plan and NYSE's other continued listing standards.

The current noncompliance with the NYSE listing standards does not affect the Company's ongoing business operations or its U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission reporting requirements. The Company is considering all available options to regain compliance with NYSE's continued listing standards.

Forward-Looking Statements

This Current Report on Form 8-K contains forward-looking statements within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "Securities Act"), and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"). These statements may relate to, but are not limited to, any statements regarding the Company's efforts and ability to regain compliance with the NYSE's continued listing standards. Forward-looking statements are inherently subject to risks and uncertainties, some of which cannot be predicted or quantified. In some cases, you can identify forward-looking statements by terminology such as "may," "will," "should," "expect," "plan," "anticipate," "could," "would," "intend," "target," "project," "contemplate," "believe," "estimate," "predict," "potential" or "continue" or the negative of these terms or other comparable terminology that concern the Company's expectations, strategy, plans or intentions. You should not put undue reliance on any forward-looking statements.

Forward-looking statements are based on information available at the time those statements are made and/or management's good faith beliefs and assumptions as of that time with respect to future events and are subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual performance or results to differ materially from those expressed in or suggested by the forward-looking statements. These risks and uncertainties include the Company's ability to develop a plan to regain compliance with the continued listing criteria of the NYSE; the NYSE's acceptance of such plan; the Company's ability to execute such plan and to continue to comply with applicable listing standards within the available cure period; risks arising from the potential suspension of trading of the Company's common stock on the NYSE, and other risks and uncertainties detailed from time to time in the Company's reports filed with the SEC. In light of these risks and uncertainties, the forward-looking events and circumstances discussed in this Current Report on Form 8-K may not occur and actual results could differ materially from those anticipated or implied in the forward-looking statements. Except as required by law, the Company does not undertake any obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statement.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1819438/000162828025015467/wk-20250324.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1819438/000162828025015467/0001628280-25-015467-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Silo Pharma, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Silo Pharma, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company focused on developing innovative treatments for rare and underserved diseases, has released its annual 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the evolving therapeutics market.

Financial Highlights

Revenues: $72,102. The company generated minimal revenues from operations, consistent with the previous year.

Gross Profit: $66,264. Gross profit remained unchanged from the prior year, reflecting stable cost management.

Operating Loss from Continuing Operations: $(4,705,694). An increase of 22.1% from the previous year, primarily due to higher research and development expenses.

Net Loss: $(4,392,880). The net loss increased by 18.7% compared to the previous year, driven by increased operating expenses.

Net Loss Per Common Share (Basic and Diluted): $1.19. A slight improvement from the previous year's $1.20 per share, despite the higher net loss.

Business Highlights

Product Development Focus: The company is concentrating on developing four main product candidates: SPC-15 for PTSD and anxiety disorders, SP-26 for fibromyalgia and chronic pain, SPC-14 for Alzheimer's disease, and SPU-16 for CNS disorders, initially targeting multiple sclerosis.

SPC-15 Development: SPC-15 is an intranasal treatment for PTSD and anxiety disorders, utilizing a novel serotonin 4 (5-HT4) receptor agonist. The company is collaborating with Columbia University and has exclusive global rights to develop and commercialize this product.

SP-26 Innovation: SP-26 is a ketamine-based injectable dissolvable polymer implant designed for chronic pain and fibromyalgia, offering a potentially safer alternative to opioids. The product is currently in preclinical research.

SPC-14 for Alzheimer's: SPC-14 targets glutamate receptor NDMAR and serotonin 5-HT4 to address cognitive and neuropsychiatric symptoms in Alzheimer's disease. The company holds exclusive global rights to develop and commercialize SPC-14.

SPU-16 for CNS Disorders: SPU-16 is a candidate targeting CNS disorders, with an initial focus on multiple sclerosis. The company has an exclusive license agreement with the University of Maryland, Baltimore, for this product.

License Agreements: The company has entered into several key license agreements, including with Columbia University for SPC-15 and with Medspray Pharma BV for a soft mist nasal spray technology as a delivery mechanism for SPC-15.

Research Collaborations: The company is actively collaborating with leading universities such as Columbia University and the University of Maryland, Baltimore, to advance its research and development efforts.

Future Outlook: The company plans to actively pursue the acquisition and development of intellectual property or technology rights to treat rare diseases, aiming to expand its business focus.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: The company is actively pursuing the acquisition and development of intellectual property or technology rights to treat rare diseases, focusing on both traditional therapeutics and psychedelic medicine. Key strategic initiatives include collaborations with Columbia University and the University of Maryland, Baltimore, to develop novel therapies for conditions such as PTSD, fibromyalgia, Alzheimer's disease, and CNS disorders. The company has also entered into a license agreement with Medspray Pharma BV for a proprietary nasal spray technology to enhance its SPC-15 product candidate.

Capital Management: The company authorized a stock repurchase plan to buy back up to $1 million of its common stock, which was extended multiple times and resulted in the repurchase of 355,710 shares for a total cost of $644,234. The company also raised capital through the sale of common stock and warrants, generating net proceeds of $3,241,628 in 2024. The company maintains a strong liquidity position with $3,905,799 in cash and cash equivalents and $3,174,724 in short-term investments as of December 31, 2024.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its strategic focus on developing and commercializing therapies for unmet medical needs, leveraging its collaborations with leading universities. It aims to submit an IND for its lead product candidate, SPC-15, in 2025 and anticipates increased research and development activities. The company believes its current cash reserves and short-term investments will be sufficient to meet its obligations for at least the next twelve months.

Challenges and Risks

Financial Position and Need for Capital: The company faces significant risks related to its financial position and need for capital. With a limited history of operations and no profitable track record, the company may sustain future losses and requires additional financing to fund operations. The lack of arrangements to raise necessary capital poses a risk of operational delays or discontinuation.

Raising Additional Capital: Raising additional capital could dilute stockholder value, restrict operations, or require relinquishing certain rights. The company may seek capital through equity offerings, debt financings, or strategic collaborations, but these could adversely affect stockholder rights and market price.

Clinical Drug Development: In the therapeutics business, clinical drug development is lengthy, expensive, and uncertain. Delays in clinical trials could prevent timely commercialization, affecting business outcomes. The company currently has no approved therapies and relies on successful regulatory approval of in-licensed product candidates.

Regulatory Risks: Regulatory risks are significant, especially concerning controlled substances like psilocybin and psilocin, which are Schedule I substances in the U.S. Compliance with DEA regulations and potential rescheduling are critical for commercialization. Changes in laws or enforcement policies could materially affect operations.

Psychedelics Industry Risks: The psychedelics industry is new and may not succeed long-term. Public controversy and adverse publicity regarding psychedelics could negatively impact market acceptance and business success.

Operational Risks: Operational risks include the company's lack of experience in commercializing therapeutic candidates, which may hinder successful market entry. The company must develop or acquire sales and marketing capabilities or partner with third parties, which could affect profitability.

Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates, which could adversely affect financial results. Management is considering hedging strategies to mitigate these risks and ensure financial stability.

SEC Filing: Silo Pharma, Inc. [ SILO ] - 10-K - Mar. 28, 2025

data.title: Cantor Equity Partners, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Cantor Equity Partners, Inc., a blank check company, has released its annual Form 10-K report, providing a comprehensive overview of its financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The company, which focuses on acquiring target businesses in industries such as financial services, healthcare, real estate services, technology, and software, completed its Initial Public Offering (IPO) in August 2024. The report outlines the company's progress and future outlook as it seeks to complete a Business Combination within the specified timeframe.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income: $1.538 million. The net income for the year ended December 31, 2024, was primarily driven by interest income on investments held in the Trust Account, partially offset by general and administrative expenses.
  • Net Income (Loss) Per Ordinary Share: Not explicitly stated. The report applies the two-class method in calculating earnings per share and allocates net income (loss) pro rata to Class A ordinary shares subject to possible redemption, nonredeemable Class A ordinary shares, and Class B ordinary shares.

Business Highlights

  • Business Strategy: The company is focused on acquiring a target business in industries that complement the experience and expertise of its management team and affiliates, particularly in financial services, healthcare, real estate services, technology, and software industries.
  • Acquisition Criteria: The company favors potential target companies with characteristics such as positive long-term growth prospects, competitive advantages, consolidation opportunities, recurring revenue, and opportunities for operational improvement.
  • Initial Public Offering: The company completed its IPO on August 14, 2024, raising $100 million through the sale of 10 million Class A ordinary shares.
  • Trust Account Management: Post-IPO, $100 million was placed in a Trust Account, which is invested in U.S. government securities or money market funds, to be used for the Business Combination or returned to shareholders if no combination is completed.
  • Business Combination Timeline: The company has until August 14, 2026, to complete a Business Combination, with the possibility of extending this period with shareholder approval.
  • Operational Expertise: The management team and affiliates have experience in sourcing, structuring, acquiring, and selling businesses, as well as fostering relationships with sellers and capital providers.
  • Potential Additional Financing: The company may seek additional funds through private offerings of debt or equity securities to complete the Business Combination, which may cause dilution to public shareholders.
  • Redemption Rights: Public shareholders have the right to redeem their shares for cash if they do not approve of the Business Combination, with the redemption price based on the Trust Account balance.
  • Competition: The company faces competition from other SPACs and entities seeking to acquire businesses with similar characteristics, which may increase the cost or complexity of completing a Business Combination.
  • Management Team Commitment: The management team is not obligated to devote a specific number of hours to the company but will allocate time as necessary to complete the Business Combination.
  • Status as a Public Company: The company believes its structure as a public entity makes it an attractive partner for target businesses, offering an alternative to traditional IPOs with potentially faster and more cost-effective processes.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Focus: The company, a blank check entity, is focused on completing a Business Combination within the specified Combination Period. It is actively searching for target businesses in the financial services, healthcare, real estate services, technology, and software industries. The company is also evaluating the potential impacts of new SEC rules on SPACs and climate-related disclosures, which may affect its ability to negotiate and complete the Business Combination.
  • Capital Management: The company raised $100 million through its Initial Public Offering and an additional $3 million through a Private Placement, with all proceeds placed in a Trust Account. It has a working capital deficit but has secured a Sponsor Loan commitment of up to $1.75 million to cover transaction costs related to the Business Combination. The company has not paid any dividends and does not plan to do so before completing the Business Combination. It has also not engaged in any equity repurchases.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates that the new SEC rules for SPACs and climate-related disclosures may increase the complexity and cost of completing the Business Combination. It plans to use its available funds for due diligence, target evaluation, and transaction structuring. The company is prepared to cease operations and liquidate if it cannot complete the Business Combination within the designated timeframe.

Challenges and Risks

  • Operational Risks: The company is a blank check company with no operating history, which presents inherent risks in selecting a suitable target for a business combination. The focus is on industries such as financial services, healthcare, real estate services, technology, and software, which may present unique challenges and opportunities.
  • Completion Risks: The company may not be able to select an appropriate target business or complete the business combination within the specified period, which could result in liquidation.
  • Personnel Risks: There is a risk of not retaining or recruiting key personnel post-business combination, which could impact operations.
  • Conflicts of Interest: Conflicts of interest may arise as officers and directors allocate their time between the company and other businesses.
  • Financing Risks: The company may face challenges in obtaining additional financing, which is crucial for completing the business combination.
  • Market Conditions: Market conditions, including geopolitical tensions and economic disruptions, could adversely affect the ability to consummate a business combination.
  • Competition: Increased competition from other SPACs may make it difficult to find attractive targets, potentially increasing costs or preventing a successful business combination.
  • Regulatory Risks: Regulatory reviews and approvals, including foreign investment regulations, may pose challenges to completing a business combination.
  • Tax Risks: The potential imposition of excise tax on redemptions could impact financial outcomes.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in interest rates and potential changes in market conditions that could affect the valuation of target businesses. The geopolitical climate, particularly the ongoing conflicts in Ukraine and the Middle East, may lead to increased volatility in capital markets, impacting the company's ability to secure a business combination.

SEC Filing: Cantor Equity Partners, Inc. [ CEP ] - 10-K - Mar. 28, 2025

data.title: Broad Street Realty, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Broad Street Realty, Inc., a company specializing in grocery-anchored and mixed-use properties in densely populated technology and education hubs, has released its annual 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $41.3 million, a decrease of 2% compared to the previous year due to lower transaction volume of leasing and the sale of two properties.
  • Operating (Loss) Gain: $(0.3) million, a decrease from a gain of $5.4 million in the previous year, primarily due to a decrease in total revenues and an increase in general and administrative expenses.
  • Net Loss: $(14.8) million, increased by 111% compared to the previous year, mainly due to a decrease in income tax benefit and total revenues.
  • Net Loss Per Share Attributable to Stockholders - Diluted: $(0.80), compared to $(0.60) in the previous year, reflecting the increased net loss.
  • FFO Attributable to Common Shares and OP Units: $0.1 million, decreased by 96% compared to the previous year, primarily due to a decrease in income tax benefit and total revenues.

Business Highlights

  • Portfolio Overview: As of December 31, 2024, the company owned 15 properties across four states, focusing on grocery-anchored and mixed-use assets in densely populated technology and education hubs in the Mid-Atlantic, Southeast, and Colorado markets.
  • Leasing Performance: The total portfolio was 92.1% leased and 89.5% occupied, with retail properties showing a 90.9% lease rate and residential properties fully leased at 100%.
  • Retail Portfolio Details: The retail portfolio, including both operating and redeveloping properties, had a total rentable square footage of 1,649,000, with anchor spaces accounting for 854,000 square feet and inline spaces for 795,000 square feet.
  • Residential Segment: Midtown Row's residential portion, serving the College of William and Mary, was 100% leased with 240 units and 620 beds, generating an annualized base rent of $9.5 million.
  • Geographical Concentration: The Washington-Baltimore-Arlington MSA accounted for 41.9% of the total GLA and 46.9% of the total annualized base rent, indicating a strong presence in this region.
  • Tenant Diversification: The top 20 tenants occupied 39.7% of the total GLA and contributed 33.9% of the total annualized base rent, with no single tenant accounting for more than 4% of the total rent.
  • Lease Expirations: Approximately 9.0% of the leases are set to expire by the end of 2025, with a weighted average remaining lease term of 5.2 years for the retail portfolio.
  • Operational Strategy: The company focuses on acquiring properties in established and developing neighborhoods leased to necessity-based tenants, with plans to expand into new markets, particularly in the Southeastern United States.
  • Property Management: The company manages or co-manages its portfolio properties and three additional properties, with a focus on increasing operating cash flows through effective leasing and property management strategies.
  • Future Outlook: The company aims to grow its portfolio through strategic acquisitions and enhance property value through repositioning, refurbishing, and redevelopment efforts, while maintaining a focus on necessity-based retail tenants.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company is focused on growing its portfolio through the acquisition of strategically positioned properties in established and developing neighborhoods, primarily leased to necessity-based tenants. It aims to expand in existing markets and new markets, including the Southeastern United States. The company also provides commercial real estate brokerage services for its own portfolio and third-party operators.
  • Capital Management: The company has engaged in several refinancing activities, including extending the maturity dates of certain loans and refinancing existing loans to secure better terms. It has a significant amount of mortgage and other indebtedness, with a focus on managing debt maturities and interest rates. The company has also entered into agreements to sell certain interests and warrants to manage its capital structure effectively. Additionally, the company is structured as an 'Up-C' corporation, conducting operations through its Operating Partnership and subsidiaries, and has a preferred equity investment agreement with a member that includes specific return and redemption terms.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to meet its long-term liquidity requirements through net cash from operations, additional secured and unsecured debt, and potentially issuing additional shares of common stock, preferred stock, or OP units. It is also exploring joint ventures or syndicated structures for future acquisitions. The company is working on refinancing certain loans and managing its capital structure to ensure compliance with debt covenants and maintain financial flexibility. The company is also addressing compliance issues related to its stock trading on the OTCQX market.

Challenges and Risks

  • Financial Performance Risks: The company has a history of net losses and expects to continue incurring losses in the near term. This may necessitate additional financing, which could be dilutive to stockholders and may not be available on favorable terms.
  • Capital and Financing Risks: The company relies heavily on external sources of capital to fund acquisitions and repay debt. Difficulties in obtaining capital could hinder the company's ability to refinance debt, make investments, or pay dividends. The limited trading market for the company's stock further constrains access to equity financing.
  • Geographic Concentration Risks: The company's properties are concentrated in the Mid-Atlantic and Colorado regions, making it vulnerable to adverse economic or regulatory developments in these areas.
  • Tenant and Lease Risks: The company depends on tenant leases for revenue. Lease terminations or tenant defaults, especially by significant tenants, could adversely affect financial performance. The company also faces competition from university-owned student housing, which may impact occupancy and rental rates.
  • Indebtedness Risks: The company has substantial indebtedness, which could limit operational flexibility and increase vulnerability to economic conditions. Secured debt obligations expose the company to foreclosure risks.
  • Operational and Management Risks: The Eagles Sub-OP Operating Agreement grants substantial rights to Fortress, which could impede operations and strategic decisions. Covenants in debt agreements may also restrict business activities.
  • Market and Economic Risks: The company is exposed to risks from changes in market conditions, such as interest rate fluctuations, competition, and economic downturns, which could impact property values and rental income.
  • Regulatory and Environmental Risks: Compliance with environmental laws and regulations could result in significant costs. The company also faces risks from natural disasters and climate change, which could damage properties and increase operating costs.
  • Cybersecurity Risks: The company is vulnerable to cybersecurity incidents, which could disrupt operations and damage reputation.
  • Stock Market Risks: The limited trading market for the company's stock and potential downgrades in market tier could affect stock liquidity and value.

data.title: Women's March Madness ratings are down without Caitlin Clark - but it's not a doom signal for the sport
data.description:
By Weston Blasi

A closer look at the early ratings numbers for the 2025 NCAA tournament suggests women's basketball is still playing - and winning - the long game

The early rounds of this year's March Madness women's basketball tournament may have seen a dip in viewership without Caitlin Clark, but plenty of other data show that the women's bracket hasn't lost its magic.

Sure, the opening round of the 2025 women's NCAA tournament averaged 367,000 viewers across all games, which is down from 469,000 viewers in the 2024 first round, or a 22% drop, as ESPN recently announced. But despite that year-over-year decline in viewership, fans shouldn't be too worried. In fact, they could actually be encouraged by the viewership numbers, sports and marketing experts say.

"The real key is not how they compare to 2024, but how they do compared to 2023," Victor Matheson, an economics professor at the College of the Holy Cross who specializes in sports, told MarketWatch. "As long as you're hitting the long-run upward trend."

And the ratings jump from 2023 is substantial: The 367,000 average viewers across all games so far in 2025 is up a massive 43% from 2023 levels, making for what ESPN said was the second-most-watched opening round in women's basketball history.

The key is to look at ratings like dots on a line graph that track something like a stock's price over time, he explained. While constant increases are nice, that kind of sustained growth with absolutely zero dips is not always realistic. Rather, having individual up and down years while maintaining an upward trajectory can be a sign of a healthy business.

While some fans may say this year's ratings dip has everything to do with Clark's departure from the University of Iowa for the WNBA, historically, sport leagues tend to naturally replace generational athletes as time goes on. Athletes like the University of Connecticut's Paige Bueckers and the University of Southern California's JuJu Watkins (who suffered an ACL injury during this year's tournament) are bright stars for the women's game moving forward.

"Caitlin Clark is a unique figure that has drawn excitement in a way that we have not seen from any other player," Matheson said. But she won't be the last one: "Every league has turnover in stars. People wrote the obituary for the NBA when [Larry] Bird and [Magic] Johnson retired - and then Michael Jordan came along. And then Jordan retires, and here comes LeBron James and Steph Curry."

Plus, the popularity of women's basketball - or any sport - doesn't rest solely on one player, even if star power can give a boost in attention. "The success of women's basketball was not Caitlin Clark on her own; it was Clark in conjunction with widely popular teams at South Carolina and LSU," Matheson added.

See: We asked AI to fill out a March Madness bracket. It picked the type of Final Four that rarely happens.

What's more, ratings are not the only judge of success, either, as the commercial side of women's March Madness remains on the rise, too.

Overall, the digital advertising spend for both the men's and women's March Madness tournaments this year was up 80%, according to Sensor Tower's 2025 Bracket Buster report.

"Capital One (COF), a title sponsor of the men's and women's NCAA tournament, increased its U.S. ad spend by 30x [year over year] in 2024, in part due to increased spend on the women's tournament," the report stated.

The Walt Disney Co. (DIS), whose ESPN channel is broadcasting the women's tournament, said total ad sales for the tournament are up 200% from last year. And commercials for the April 6 women's championship game - some of which are going for $1 million apiece - have been sold out for months.

In another sign of growth, the women's tournament had 67 sponsors last year, but added 45 new ones this year for a total of 112 total advertisers, per an AdWeek analysis. And the sponsors that stayed increased their budgets by 81%.

Broadcasters sell rights to commercials or marketing campaigns well in advance of marquee events, so media partners like ESPN didn't see major commercial benefits for last year's record-breaking women's tournament at the time. Instead, the following year after a big ratings boosts is when broadcasters tend to cash in on more expensive ad campaigns.

See: Women's March Madness teams are being paid for the first time - but they're splitting $200 million less than the men

And many women's players are also seeing their individual brands rise on social media. Athletes like Bueckers and Watkins are among the top earners when it comes to name, image and likeness (NIL) brand deals. Many of them have secured well over $1 million in deals during their careers so far with brands like Nike (NKE), Bose and Taco Bell (YUM).

"I would not be surprised if the percentage of people who know Paige is about as high as the percentage of people who know any individual on the men's side. Maybe [Duke University's] Copper Flagg, but maybe not even him," Matheson said.

And at least one women's player has a deal with a major financial company, too.

Louisiana State University's Flau'jae Johnson signed a brand deal with financial-data and consumer-services company Experian (UK:EXPN) (EXPGY), whose commercials feature prominently during ad breaks for the men's and women's games. As part of the deal, Experian said it will forgive $100,000 worth of consumer debt for each win that Johnson's LSU team gets in March. That windfall is currently sitting at $200,000.

"Each game is an opportunity to inspire not just on the court, but to champion economic education that can transform communities," Johnson said in a statement to MarketWatch.

What's more, Deloitte's "Beyond the billion-dollar barrier: Charting the next phase of growth" report notes that women's athletes at big events like the Olympics make up a greater percentage of social engagement compared to men's athletes.

"Despite only 43% of the competition news coverage focused on women athletes, they generated 53% of total engagement across social content, showcasing the ability of women athletes to connect with both traditional and new fans," authors Jennifer Haskel, Pete Giorgio, Zoe Burton and Lizzie Tantam wrote in the Deloitte report.

The upward trend for women's college basketball and women's sports overall comes as women's March Madness teams are getting paid for the first time in 2025. The men's teams have been paid for their participation in the tournament since 1991.

Unfortunately for players, those funds go to their schools' athletic programs and conferences, and not to individuals, because NCAA policy prohibits payments directly to athletes. The payments, which are essentially prize money, are calculated based on how far a team makes it in the tournament.

The men's tournament will distribute approximately $216 million this year, while the women's tournament will send out just $15 million.

See: These 10 college athletes are making over $1 million a year in NIL deals

-Weston Blasi

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Wheat Futures Fall on Black Sea NegotiationsDaily Grain Highlights
data.description:
By Kirk Maltais

  • Wheat for May delivery fell 0.7% to $5.28 1/2 a bushel on the Chicago Board of Trade on Friday, with analysts and traders anticipating that negotiations to temporarily halt fighting will allow export shipments to pass through the Black Sea.
  • Soybeans for May delivery rose 0.5% to $10.17 1/2 a bushel.
  • Corn for May delivery rose 0.7% to $4.56 1/4 a bushel.

HIGHLIGHTS

Wheat in Retreat: The decline in wheat prices led the CBOT lower as negotiations to halt hostilities between Russia and Ukraine weigh on prices. "Though the resumption of the grain accord brokered by Turkey is uncertain, the possibility of increased Black Sea output and exports is also seen as a negative price driver, especially towards wheat," Ocean State Research's Joel Karlin said. The Black Sea Grain Initiative came into force in July 2022, and lasted for about a year.

Fear Factor: Worries about how reciprocal tariffs expected on April 2 will affect demand is weighing on the agricultural complex. "The uncertainty of it all has fear as the dominant factor in the markets heading into the weekend," said Arlan Suderman of StoneX in a note. In particular, Suderman is watching for retaliatory tariffs from Mexico, which would have a big effect on U.S. grain exports. "Retaliatory tariffs by Mexico on Ag commodities could crush demand for vital commodities."

INSIGHT

Drought May Ease: Data tracking dryness in U.S. crop-growing areas is gaining importance with analysts and traders, with extreme drought conditions spreading in the Northern Plains and in Texas, according to data from the U.S. Drought Monitor on Thursday. While the data show expanded areas of drought across the Corn Belt, rainfall is expected to hit the Eastern Corn Belt soon. "7-Day precipitation accumulation has been boosted to 2-4 inches east of the Mississippi River," Price Futures Group's Daniel Flynn said in a note. "This matches what's needed to ease/end drought there, and so is viewed as beneficial."

Unnerving Data: A hotter-than-expected February core PCE reading sparked new concerns about stagflation and the role tariffs may play going forward. "We are moving in the wrong direction and the concern is that tariffs threaten higher prices, which mean the inflation prints are going to remain hot," ING Economics said in a note. "This will constrain the Fed's ability to deliver further interest rate cuts." These concerns have pushed commodity futures into the red across the board, except for precious metals and copper, with gold surging above the $3,100 per troy ounce.

AHEAD

  • The USDA will release its weekly Grain Export Inspections report at 11 a.m. ET Monday.
  • The USDA will release its yearly Prospective Planting report at noon ET Monday.
  • The USDA will release its quarterly Grain Stocks report at noon ET Monday.

data.title: OpenAI's Latest Funding Round Comes With a $20 Billion Catch
data.description:
By Berber Jin and Deepa Seetharaman

OpenAI is finalizing a $40 billion funding round in what would be one of the largest startup fundraises of all time. The catch: The ChatGPT maker could lose out on half that amount if it fails to meet a key condition.

In order to receive the full sum, the nonprofit has to successfully restructure into a for-profit company by the end of the year, according to people familiar with the situation. If it doesn't, its lead investor — SoftBank — can pare back the funding round's size to $20 billion.

The funding stipulation adds pressure to OpenAI's ongoing restructuring talks, which require the blessing of its largest shareholder, Microsoft. It is also subject to review by the California attorney general.

The details

SoftBank is expected to contribute up to $30 billion to the round and is syndicating the rest to other investors, including Microsoft, some of the people said. The funding round would value OpenAI at up to $300 billion, the Journal previously reported.

The OpenAI funding is divided across two tranches. The company will initially receive $10 billion and can receive up to another $30 billion at the end of the year if it completes the for-profit change, the people said. The second tranche would be dialed back to $10 billion if the restructuring doesn't take place by then.

The context

Investors started to demand that OpenAI transition to a for-profit company after Sam Altman was briefly removed as chief executive in late 2023. The board said at the time that Altman had failed to be consistently honest in his communications but didn't elaborate, leading OpenAI's investors to argue that the unconventional nonprofit structure carried too many risks. He was reinstated days later.

OpenAI told investors in its fall funding round that it would return their money if the restructuring wasn't completed within two years. The change faces major hurdles, including fending off a lawsuit from rival Elon Musk trying to block the restructuring. OpenAI also needs approval from its largest shareholder Microsoft, and the tech giant has made clear that it won't do so without a fight.

The stakes

Should OpenAI fail to become a for-profit, it would be difficult for the company to continue raising the billions of dollars needed to fund its money-losing operations. Investors are so far backing OpenAI with the expectation that their current stake — in the form of convertible notes — will turn into traditional equity after the restructuring.

Only receiving half of the $40 billion would squeeze OpenAI's already strained finances.

The startup is losing billions of dollars a year to train and operate its AI systems and pay for top-notch researchers, and it has committed $18 billion for Stargate, a separate data-center construction project blessed by President Trump. SoftBank has separately committed another $18 billion to Stargate.

In addition to its computing costs, OpenAI has content-licensing deals with companies like The Wall Street Journal's parent company, News Corp, that give it data that can be used to train its models.

Write to Berber Jin at berber.jin@wsj.com and Deepa Seetharaman at deepa.seetharaman@wsj.com

data.title: CoreWeave Stock Ends Flat on First Day of Trading After Scaled-Back IPO
data.description:
By Corrie Driebusch and Deepa Seetharaman

It was supposed to be one of the splashiest IPOs of the year. Now CoreWeave's stock-market debut is turning into a high-profile stumble for both the AI industry and new public listings.

The startup that rents out access to Nvidia chips priced its initial public offering below expectations late Thursday and then opened even lower on Friday, a sign of flagging investor enthusiasm for artificial-intelligence companies as well as the chilly state of the U.S. market for new listings.

Later Friday, the stock had climbed above the original IPO price of $40, but remained lower than the company had been hoping.

That is a sharp contrast from just last year. Investor mania for all things AI drove up shares of Nvidia, making the chip maker the world's most valuable publicly traded company for the first time (a title it has since ceded). CoreWeave raised money in May at a $19 billion valuation, nearly triple its price just five months prior. Its backers include OpenAI and Fidelity.

The sheen has since worn off. The New Jersey-based company priced its offering at $40 a share, well below the $47 to $55 range it was targeting for a fully-diluted valuation of up to $32 billion. The lowered price reflects a valuation of approximately $23 billion. The company also sold fewer shares than planned.

"Obviously there are macro headwinds, AI headwinds," said Chief Executive Mike Intrator. "Everybody likes a higher price. But at the end of the day we IPO'd."

Friday, CoreWeave opened for trading even lower at $39 before rising above $40. The stock is listed on the Nasdaq stock exchange under the symbol CRWV.

Investors pushed back on the price tag during CoreWeave's IPO roadshow, citing the company's high costs and debt load. The uncertainty echoes the broad concerns many hold about the sustainability of the AI boom, for which the world's biggest companies are spending hundreds of billions of dollars.

Some of the issues are specific to CoreWeave's business, including its concentrated customer base. Microsoft accounted for nearly two-thirds of the company's revenue in 2024.

CoreWeave operates a network of data centers that house the chips and equipment the company rents out to clients, who in turn create and deploy AI systems. The company brought in $1.9 billion in revenue in 2024, yet still lost $863 million, due in part to high operating expenses.

Its business took off in 2023 when AI startups were taking any route possible to obtain specialized graphics chips, or GPUs, that companies use to build and train AI systems that they then sell to companies. One founder at the time compared looking for GPUs to hunting for " toilet paper during the pandemic."

The supply shortage has since subsided, and companies say it is comparatively easy to buy the required chips. Renting a GPU for an hour cost about $5.50 in mid-2023; it's now $1.55, said Evan Conrad, chief executive of San Francisco Compute, a market for GPUs.

CoreWeave's IPO pricing is likely to disappoint a host of private companies that had been considering U.S. listings.

Bankers had hoped 2025 would be a return to normal for the IPO market after many private companies refrained from going public the last three years. Ticketing marketplace StubHub and fintech company Klarna are among those waiting in the wings with springtime IPO plans. Chime Financial and Medline Industries are also expected to go public later this year. A poor showing by CoreWeave could delay — or derail — their plans.

It isn't the first big IPO that hasn't lived up to expectations this year.

In January, gas exporter Venture Global slashed its price by more than 40% from its initial goal in the biggest offering of the year. Its shares still fell in opening trading. At the time, bankers told clients the poor showing was company-specific and not reflective of broader investor demand for new listings.

The second biggest IPO of the year, cybersecurity firm SailPoint, also fell in its stock-market debut last month.

Both SailPoint and Venture Global stocks remain below their IPO prices, meaning if investors bought shares in those offerings they are sitting on big losses.

Stock-market volatility isn't helping. Swings in the broader stock market make it tricky for investors to value companies. The up-and-down could give companies cold feet, by sapping confidence about their business prospects.

Write to Corrie Driebusch at corrie.driebusch@wsj.com and Deepa Seetharaman at deepa.seetharaman@wsj.com

data.title: PureTech Systems Integrates PurifAI with IMMIX’s Monitoring Platforms to Transform Video Alarm Validation
data.description:
PHOENIX, March 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — PureTech Systems Inc., a leader in geospatial AI-boosted video analytics for wide-area perimeter and border security, is proud to announce the integration of its patented, award-winning SaaS solution, PurifAI™, with IMMIX’s CS & GS video monitoring platforms. This integration delivers a breakthrough in video alarm validation, significantly reducing nuisance alarms while enhancing the efficiency of both self-monitoring and central monitoring operations.

PurifAI is a cloud-based service designed to intelligently process video alarms from diverse sources—including cameras, DVRs, NVRs, third-party video analytics, and motion sensors. Leveraging PureTech’s advanced AI-powered analytics, PurifAI filters out nuisance alarms, ensuring only verified security events are forwarded to IMMIX’s monitoring platforms. Non-actionable alarms are logged for reference, reducing operator distractions and dramatically improving operational effectiveness.

Key Benefits of PurifAI Integration:

  • Near-Elimination of Nuisance Alarms – Operators can focus on critical threats instead of nuisance alarms.
  • Scalability for Growth – Enables video monitoring centers to handle more sites without increasing staffing needs.
  • Lower Operational Costs – Reduces time and resources spent on unnecessary alarms, improving cost-efficiency.
  • Seamless Compatibility – Works with existing video infrastructure (NVRs, DVRs, cameras) without requiring additional hardware.
  • Enhanced Operator Productivity – Fewer interruptions lead to better efficiency, job satisfaction, and customer retention.

"We are excited to integrate our award-winning, patented AI-driven video analytics into IMMIX’s monitoring platform," said Larry Bowe, CEO of PureTech Systems Inc. "PurifAI is a game-changer for central monitoring operations, offering superior filtering accuracy that eliminates nuisance alarms. This empowers monitoring centers to enhance service reliability, effortlessly scale operations, and significantly cut operating costs."

About PureTech Systems Inc.

PureTech Systems Inc. is a privately owned company established in 2004 that develops, markets, and supports its patented location-based AI-boosted video analytics software, PureActiv®, for real time safety and security applications and our SaaS-based PurifAI™. Their primary emphasis is on autonomous perimeter intrusion detection and classification of ground and aerial targets for country borders, coastlines, facility perimeters, and critical infrastructures (pipelines, railroads, dams, bridges, ports, utilities, power plants, military bases, and airports).

To find out more about PureTech Systems Inc. visit our website at www.puretechsystems.com, call 602-424-9842 or email info@PureTechSystems.com.

About IMMIX

Immix is a global provider of software that improves the ability of commercial central stations and monitoring centers to manage and respond to security events as well as deliver a variety of video-centric managed services to customers. The software is deployed successfully across a wide array of environments servicing commercial, residential, and enterprise markets. Immix supports the largest third-party product integration library in the industry, enabling ease of deployment and system administration for a broad spectrum of organizations across the globe. Immix® is UL Certified in the USA and BS8418 compliant in Europe. For more information, visit www.immixprotect.com.

data.title: Trump pardoned BitMEX co-founders, White House official says
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has pardoned the three co-founders of cryptocurrency exchange BitMEX, a White House official said on Friday.

CNBC was the first to report earlier in the day that on Thursday, Trump granted pardons to Benjamin Delo, Arthur Hayes and Samuel Reed, who had pled guilty to money-laundering charges.

The pardons come at a time of optimism in the crypto industry about looser regulation under Trump, who courted crypto donors during his campaign and promised support.

Prosecutors accused BitMEX and founders Delo, Hayes and Reed of willfully violating the Bank Secrecy Act between 2015 and 2020 by failing to adopt anti-money-laundering and "know your customer" programs, Reuters reported in January.

BitMEX did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment.

The president has also pardoned Trevor Milton, founder of bankrupt electric and hydrogen-powered truckmaker Nikola , who was convicted of fraud.

data.title: RBC Maintains Tesla Price Target Despite Forecasted Delivery Decline in First Quarter
data.description:
RBC Capital reiterated its Outperform rating on Tesla (TSLA, Financials) and held its price target at $320, despite expectations of lower deliveries in the first quarter of 2025. The reaffirmation comes as analysts at Stifel projected deliveries of approximately 364,000 vehicles.

The main reasons for the delivery shortfall are planned production stops in January and February for an update to the Model Y and possible buyers waiting to buy until the second quarter, when a cheaper model is expected to come out. So far this year, Tesla's stock price has dropped 30.5%.

Weak performance around the world is also putting pressure on delivery estimates. People in China expect a 47.8% drop in sales in the first quarter compared to the previous quarter. This is because January and February numbers will be 57% lower than October and November numbers. Weekly insurance data from March shows that rates will continue to go down until the end of the quarter. Deliveries are expected to drop 19.9% from one quarter to the next in Europe. The first two months of the year saw a 33% drop.

RBC said it is still confident in Tesla's long-term market position, even though its goal price went down. The company will officially report shipping numbers for the first quarter next week.

Other experts also released new figures for delivery and price. Canaccord Genuity kept its "Buy" rating and set a price target of $404. It also changed its delivery estimate to 362,000 cars. Piper Sandler maintained an Overweight rating and a $450 goal, saying that the main problem is supply-side delays rather than problems with demand.

The company is also being looked into by the FBI because of reports of violent threats. The FBI has called it domestic terrorism, which makes Tesla's already high risks even higher.

Elon Musk, the CEO of Tesla, recently admitted that taxes on imported cars have made things harder for the company. Piper Sandler said that new projects, like a base for robo-taxis, would help the company grow in the long run.

data.title: Markforged 4Q Rev $22.4M >MKFG
Lotus Technology to Report Unaudited Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Financial Results on April 22, 2025
data.description:
NEW YORK, March 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”) , a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced that it will report its unaudited fourth quarter and full year 2024 financial results on April 22, 2025, before market opening in the United States.

The Company’s management will host an earnings conference call at 8:00 AM U.S. Eastern Time on Tuesday, April 22, 2025 (14:00 Central European Time / 20:00 China Standard Time on the same day).

There will be a live audio webcast and replay available following completion of the call on the Company’s investor relations website at https://ir.group-lotus.com/news-events/events/

For participants who wish to view the live webcast, please register at https://edge.media-server.com/mmc/p/mdbmd5h5

For participants who wish to join the call, please complete online registration prior to the scheduled call start time using the link provided below. Upon registration, participants will receive a confirmation email with conference call access information, including dial-in numbers and a unique PIN.

Participant online registration link: https://register-conf.media-server.com/register/BI6f2e717ea0604566888b1c361deb51c5

About Lotus Technology Inc.

Lotus Technology Inc. has operations across the UK, the EU and China. The Company is dedicated to delivering luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicles, with a focus on world-class R&D in next-generation automobility technologies such as electrification, digitalisation and more. For more information about Lotus Technology Inc., please visit www.group-lotus.com.
